name: byte_indexing_with_drain_delay
title: Byte-array indexing on extern s32, with sw drained from jr-delay
summary:
Two issues compound when the function does `BASE[i] |= mask` (or `&= ~mask`) where BASE is `extern s32`:
  1. m2c emits `*(&BASE + (i * 4))` which GCC scales by sizeof(s32)=4 again, producing a *16 multiplier; fix by rewriting as `(&BASE)[i]` (proper array indexing).
  2. With the C now correct, GCC schedules the closing `sw <result>, ofs(<base>)` into the jr ra delay slot, but target keeps a `nop` there. Apply `drain_delay` to undo the schedule.
proof: func_80078B3C (commit 2353f22) and func_80078B70 (commit 1c5d90c) -- bit-set and bit-clear siblings, identical recipe.

structural: m2c base.c contains `*(&D_<addr> + (<expr> * 4))` or `&D_<addr> + <expr> * <const>` with D_<addr> declared `extern s32`
after byte-arithmetic C fix, gen_regfix shows: matches~10, reg_diffs=0, structural=3, instruction_swaps=1
the swap signature is jr/j followed by sw at the next index

rule_skeleton.instruction: Replace `*(&D_<sym> + (v * 4))` (or `(v * N) + &D_<sym>`) with `(&D_<sym>)[v]` -- forces single byte-aware multiplication.

rule_skeleton.comment: move sw out of jr delay slot

notes: Find JR_IDX from `dc.sh dump-text <func>` -- it's the index of `j $31`, with the `sw` at JR_IDX+1.
If gen_regfix reports `instruction_swaps: 1` after the C edit, this recipe almost certainly applies.
near_miss_attempt.py auto-detects this pattern (in the `drain_delay` recipe step).
